Hi, and welcome to Line Sheet\xe2\x80\x99s first Inner Circle-exclusive issue. Today\xe2\x80\x99s email is about the future of Herm\xc3\xa8s, one of my all-time favorite topics of conversation. Word in Paris is that the world\xe2\x80\x99s most worshiped luxury firm is prepping for the launch of a new category. More on that below, plus a couple quick thoughts from me.

Jan 10, 2025

PREVIEW VERSION

For full access to Puck Inner Circle content, upgrade or subscribe today.

Lauren Sherman

Hi, and welcome to our first Inner Circle-exclusive issue. As a reminder, Line Sheet has expanded to five days a week , but the Thursday edition of this private email is only available to our highest tier of membership. We\xe2\x80\x99re including a preview version here today, but upgrade here to read the whole thing going forward. I\xe2\x80\x99m grateful for your support and also believe that this product is very much worth it.

Thanks to everyone who reached out as we navigate these horrendous fires. My family and I are okay, and I hope you\xe2\x80\x99re okay, too. One big lesson, if you want to get philosophical, is that the lack of local newsgathering makes disasters like these even more difficult to manage. If you are a media executive, it\xe2\x80\x99s never been a better time to crack this local news thing.

Today\xe2\x80\x99s email is about the future of Herm\xc3\xa8s, one of my all-time favorite topics of conversation. Word in Paris is that the world\xe2\x80\x99s most worshiped luxury firm is prepping for the launch of a new category. More on that below, plus a couple quick thoughts from me. And if you need a true distraction from the tragedy at hand, might I suggest checking out tomorrow\xe2\x80\x99s episode of Fashion People with the inimitable Christopher Kane , whose recent collaboration with the London-based Self-Portrait made me miss seeing his wares on the runway. We go pretty deep, and I hope you enjoy it. Listen here and here .


Mentioned in this issue: Herm\xc3\xa8s, Nad\xc3\xa8ge Vanhee , Axel Dumas , Chanel, Matthieu Blazy , Versace, the Agnelli family, Mayhoola, Kering, Rick Caruso , Palisades Village, Jonathan Anderson , Loewe, Bottega Veneta, Bruno Pavlovsky , Chris Black , Patrick Radden Keefe , Ozempic, Legos, Babygirl , Daniel - Yaw Miller , and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

Three Things You Should Know\xe2\x80\xa6

  • Back to big brand dominance? : Over the past few years, the smaller big fashion brands have dominated the industry\xe2\x80\x99s attention: not only Jonathan Anderson at Loewe and Matthieu Blazy at Bottega Veneta, but also...

UPGRADE TO INNER CIRCLE

Herm\xc3\xa8s Goes Up, Up, Up Market

The legendary, family-controlled leather goods house is contemplating an unprecedented and decidedly old-school move: couture. Do the costs and craft required for this rarefied market make sense for the already stellar quarter-trillion-dollar business?

Lauren Sherman

In fashion, we often say that there is Herm\xc3\xa8s, and then there is everyone else. The 186-year-old firm doesn\xe2\x80\x99t play by the rules of luxury, it makes them\xe2\x80\x94setting standards in terms of pricing, distribution, and quality, etcetera. Herm\xc3\xa8s famously doesn\xe2\x80\x99t have a marketing department or hire celebrities to front its advertising campaigns.

Herm\xc3\xa8s also refuses to charge what the market would bear for its priciest handbags. That\xe2\x80\x99s a pointed tactic that has nestled the company, controlled by the same family for six generations, in the good graces of consumers while still profiting handsomely. Herm\xc3\xa8s made about $15 billion in 2023, which is peanuts compared to LVMH\xe2\x80\x99s $86 billion, but it had a comparatively advantaged 43.49 percent operating profit compared to LVMH\xe2\x80\x99s 26.5 percent. This spread explains, in part, how its $244 billion market cap is around 75 percent of LVMH\xe2\x80\x99s own $329 billion valuation.

Since Herm\xc3\xa8s does what it wants, my interest was piqued when I heard recently that the company was plotting an expansion into a category historically devoid of new entrants...

Puck\xe2\x80\x99s daily art market email, anchored by industry expert Marion Maneker, offers unparalleled access to the mega-auctions and galleries, elite buyers and sellers, and the power players who run this opaque world. Wall Power also features Julie Davich, a veteran of Christie\xe2\x80\x99s and Sotheby\xe2\x80\x99s, who provides unique insights into how the business really works.
